subject: Sociology
title: Sociology of Everyday Life
description: A fast breakdown of Sociology of Everyday Life for builders.

hook.text: You hold a door open for a stranger and smile. They nod back. A tiny ritual just reinforced the entire social order.

buildup.text: Erving Goffman studied everyday interactions as performances. We all manage impressions for different audiences.

discovery.text: Front stage is your public self. Backstage is where you relax, complain, and drop the act.

twist.text: Social media collapses front and backstage. Your boss, mom, and friends all see the same feed.

climax.text: Even casual moments follow scripts. Sociology reveals that almost nothing you do in public is truly spontaneous.

punchline.text: Every interaction is a performance. The audience just changes.

quiz.question: What did Goffman mean by front stage and backstage?
options: Public self-presentation vs. private relaxed behavior
Literal theater stages in community settings
